title: alchemilla pubescens subsp. montana
intro: Alchemilla pubescens subsp. montana, also known as the Mountain Lady's Mantle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and roots have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, burns, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and promote healing. Herbal preparations of Alchemilla pubescens subsp. montana can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
